evals.prompt: Use $pr-status on the current branch and tell me if it can land.
expectations: Resolves the PR from the current branch without creating or editing a PR.
Reads draft state, merge state, review decision, checks, and head/base.
Returns one readiness status with blockers and evidence.
Names the smallest next command without mutating local or remote state.

evals.prompt: This PR has failing checks and requested changes. Use $pr-status to tell me what is blocking it.
expectations: Classifies the PR as needing repair instead of ready to land.
Separates failing checks from review-request blockers.
Does not patch code, comment, resolve threads, or merge.
Recommends $repair-pr for actionable same-PR failures or comments.

evals.prompt: Use $pr-status for PR #42 and include whether unresolved conversations may block merge.
expectations: Uses PR metadata and GraphQL thread state when conversation resolution matters.
Marks thread state unknown if GitHub does not expose enough information.
Explains whether conversation state is a known blocker or an uncertainty.

evals.prompt: Use $pr-status to check whether the PR diff still matches the base branch before landing.
expectations: Compares PR files and local merge-base diff when scope or base matters.
Keeps the workflow read-only apart from metadata fetches.
Routes ready PRs to $land-pr and scope problems to a user decision or $send-it.
